reasoning: As of May 11, 2025, there has been no reported human-to-human (H2H) transmission of H5N1 locally or globally by the CDC. The US outbreak linked to dairy cattle has caused ~70 human cases so far, but robust surveillance shows all cases have been animal-to-human. Furthermore, the most prominent suspected case of H2H spread (the Missouri cluster in late 2024) was definitively ruled out by CDC serological testing. Sequencing of recent human strains confirms the virus retains strong avian-receptor binding without the key mutations needed for efficient mammalian transmission. While historically there are rare instances of limited, non-sustained H2H H5N1 transmission (e.g. within families), none have occurred during the current intense outbreak despite ample opportunities. With only 7.5 months remaining in the resolution window, the window for a H2H event to occur and be officially reported by the CDC is narrowing. I am forecasting 7%, half of the early May community prediction of 14% (which was likely elevated due to uncertainty over unconfirmed clusters that have since resolved negative).
